title: Reading and Use of English – Part 3
description: For questions 17–24, read the text below. Use the word given in CAPITALS at the end of some of the lines to form a word that fits in the gap in the same line.

content.text: In recent years, there has been a (0) SIGNIFICANT (SIGNIFY) increase in the awareness of mental health issues. Many (17) ORGANISATIONS (ORGANISE) are now providing more (18) SUPPORTIVE (SUPPORT) environments for their employees. This (19) RECOGNITION (RECOGNIZE) that well-being is as important as physical health is (20) EXTREMELY (EXTREME) beneficial. (21) UNFORTUNATELY (FORTUNE), some people still face (22) DISCRIMINATION (DISCRIMINATE) due to the stigma attached to these conditions. To combat this, (23) EDUCATIONAL (EDUCATION) campaigns aim to promote (24) ACCEPTANCE (ACCEPT) and understanding. By fostering open (25) DISCUSSIONS (DISCUSS), we can ensure that those in need feel comfortable seeking help. Ultimately, a more (26) COMPASSIONATE (COMPASSION) society will lead to better outcomes for everyone. As we continue to break down barriers and challenge outdated beliefs, the focus on mental health will become an integral part of our daily lives, ensuring that everyone has the opportunity to thrive and reach their full potential in a safe and supportive community.

example.text: In recent years, there has been a (0) SIGNIFICANT (SIGNIFY) increase...
example.explanation: Adjective required to modify 'increase'; SIGNIFY → SIGNIFICANT.

questions.explanation: Adjective required to modify 'environments'; SUPPORT → SUPPORTIVE.
Adverb required to modify 'beneficial'; EXTREME → EXTREMELY.
Adverb with a negative prefix required at the start of the sentence; FORTUNE → UNFORTUNATELY.
Noun required after the verb 'face'; DISCRIMINATE → DISCRIMINATION.
Adjective required to modify 'campaigns'; EDUCATION → EDUCATIONAL.
